user.title: Connect to VerdiGrow
user.description: VerdiGrow URL (e.g. http://192.168.1.7:8000 direct, or your https proxy URL) and the API token from VerdiGrow → Garden Setup → API access. Uncheck Verify SSL for a self-signed/untrusted https cert.

data.verify_ssl: Verify SSL certificate (uncheck for self-signed / untrusted proxy certs)

abort.already_configured: This VerdiGrow instance is already configured.

menu_options.map_area: Link an HA area to VerdiGrow

add_mapping.title: Add a sensor mapping — step 1 of 2
add_mapping.description: Pick which linked HA area to work in. This filters the sensor list on the next screen to the entities in that HA area.

map_entity.title: Add a sensor mapping — step 2 of 2
map_entity.description: Sensors in {area}. Pick one HA sensor, the VerdiGrow target(s) it feeds (areas, containers or plants in this HA area), and which metric it is.

data.targets: VerdiGrow target(s) — area, container or plant

map_area.title: Link an HA area to VerdiGrow
map_area.description: Pick a Home Assistant area and the VerdiGrow container(s)/area(s) it covers. This scopes the sensor list so the picker only shows sensors from linked HA areas.

abort.cannot_connect: Could not reach VerdiGrow to load containers/areas.
abort.no_mappings: There are no mappings to remove yet.
abort.map_area_first: Link an HA area to VerdiGrow first (so the sensor list can be scoped).
abort.no_entities_in_areas: No sensors found in the linked HA area(s).
